findings.summary: RC filter overcounting: 12 detected but only 6 are true signal-path filters; Q1 (SQD50P04-13L_T4GE3) classified as is_pchannel=false, but it is a P-Channel MOSFET; LM340AT (U1) classified as topolo...

correct.description: The component description explicitly states 'P-Channel 40 V 50A (Tc)' and the part number encodes P-channel in its name ('50P04'). The lib_id uses a custom library (SQD50P04-13L_T4GE3:SQD50P04-13L_T4GE3) rather than the standard Transistor_FET: namespace, which appears to prevent the analyzer from detecting P-channel status. The analyzer should also check the component description field for 'P-Channel' or 'P-channel' keywords when the lib_id is not in a recognized namespace. This misclassification also causes an incorrect topology reading: the circuit shows drain_net=+BATT and source_net=VCC which is consistent with a P-channel high-side switch, not an N-channel device.

The LM340AT uses the KiCad lib_id 'Regulator_Linear:L7805' which is the 78xx series fixed positive voltage regulator. The 78xx/LM340 family is NOT an LDO — it requires approximately 2V of dropout voltage at full load. True LDOs typically achieve <300-500mV dropout. The analyzer classifies all Regulator_Linear parts as 'LDO' topology, which is misleading for classic fixed-dropout regulators. The output_rail is correctly identified as +5V.

incorrect.description: Each optocoupler/Schmitt-trigger input has both a pull-up resistor (e.g. R24 from +3.3V) and a series resistor (e.g. R30 from signal) both connecting to the same RC node (e.g. IN_N2 with C17 to GND). The analyzer detects both the pull-up R and the series R as separate RC filters with the same capacitor, resulting in 12 detections instead of 6. The 6 spurious entries all have a power rail (+3.3V) as their input_net, while the 6 genuine signal-path filters have unnamed nets as their input. The genuine filters are: R28-C18, R30-C17, R34-C16, R36-C15, R38-C14, R40-C13.

missed.description: The design contains three DRV8872DDA integrated H-bridge brushed DC motor drivers (lib_id: Driver_Motor:DRV8872DDA), yet signal_analysis.bridge_circuits is empty (0 entries). The drivers are correctly identified as 'motor driver' in ic_pin_analysis.function, so the functional identification is present. However, bridge_circuits remains empty because that detector targets discrete transistor H-bridge topologies rather than integrated motor driver ICs. A user relying on bridge_circuits to find all motor drive stages would miss all three DRV8872 instances. Ideally, integrated motor driver ICs from the Driver_Motor: library family should also appear in bridge_circuits.
